seoTitle: How to Add a Column (Field) in Airtable
metaDescription: Add a new column to your Airtable base by choosing a field type, from single select to formulas, and configuring its display settings.
intro: Airtable calls columns 'fields,' and adding one gives you access to specialized types like formulas, attachments, or single-select tags rather than just plain text. This guide walks through adding a new field to your table and configuring it.

steps.heading: Open your target table
steps.body: Go to your Airtable workspace and open the base and table where you want to add a column.

steps.heading: Find the plus icon
steps.body: Scroll to the far right of your existing columns to locate the add-field button.

steps.heading: Click to open the field panel
steps.body: Click the plus icon to open the new field customization panel.

steps.heading: Choose a field type and name it
steps.body: Type your column title and select from the library of field types, such as single select, attachments, formulas, or numbers.

steps.heading: Configure and save
steps.body: Adjust any specific properties or default values, save to deploy the column, and drag it into position if you want to reorder your layout.

faq.question: What field types can I choose from in Airtable?
faq.answer: Airtable offers a wide library of field types including text, single select, attachments, formulas, numbers, dates, and linked records to other tables.

faq.question: Can I change a column's field type after creating it?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit an existing field's type later, though converting between very different types may require reformatting the existing data.

faq.question: How do I hide a column without deleting it?
faq.answer: You can hide fields from a specific view without removing the underlying data, which keeps your grid focused while preserving the column.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many columns I can add?
faq.answer: Airtable allows a large number of fields per table, though very high field counts can affect performance depending on your plan.
